Day 100: Day 100 Assessment - 3rd Grade BedrockELA

Page 1

Lesson 100

Assessment Day

The Secret Garden

Page 2

Assessment Instructions

Today is an assessment day! You'll be tested on:

  • Vocabulary: 10 words from Days 96-100
  • Reading Comprehension: Questions about the chapters
  • Grammar: Adjectives and Adjective Order
  • Writing: Opinion Writing

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

Tip: Take your time and do your best work!

Page 3

Vocabulary Assessment

Write the definition for each word. Use complete sentences.

1. delightful

2. humble

3. radiant

4. miracle

5. terrific

6. solemn

7. earnest

8. whisper

9. struggle

10. comfort

Page 4

Reading Comprehension Test

Answer these questions about the chapters you read this week:

Question 1:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 2:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 3:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 4:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 5:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Page 5

Grammar Assessment

Adjectives and Adjective Order

Write three sentences with multiple adjectives describing characters or places from the chapter.

Page 6

Writing Assessment

Opinion Writing

Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

Requirements: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

Page 7

Assessment Complete!

Great work on your assessment! You've completed 5 full days of The Secret Garden.

See you on Day 101!

Day 101: Lesson 101 - 3rd Grade BedrockELA

Page 1

Lesson 101

3rd Grade ELA

The Secret Garden

Chapter 11

Page 2

Welcome to Lesson 101!

Today you will:

  • Read from The Railway Children
  • Learn 2 new vocabulary words
  • Read informational text: "Related Non-Fiction"
  • Practice grammar: Past Progressive Tense
  • Write: Informative Writing
  • Explore: Similes and Metaphors

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

Page 3

Vocabulary Words

persecuted

Look up this word in a dictionary, then write the definition in your own words:

0 / 10 words
exile

Look up this word in a dictionary, then write the definition in your own words:

0 / 10 words

Watch for these words in today's reading!

Page 4

Reading: The Indian in the Cupboard

Chapter 11

Page 1 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Indian in the Cupboard - Chapter 11, Part 1

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Book: The Railway Children by E. Nesbit

Page 5

Page 2 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Indian in the Cupboard - Chapter 11, Part 2

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 6

Page 3 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Indian in the Cupboard - Chapter 11, Part 3

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 7

Page 4 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Indian in the Cupboard - Chapter 11, Part 4

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 8

Page 5 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Indian in the Cupboard - Chapter 11, Part 5

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What was Heidi's reaction when she saw the beautiful mountain scenery from her window?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

Why do you think Heidi was so eager to explore the mountains and go outside?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

How do you think Heidi's life in the mountains will be different from her life in the city?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 11]

Page 11

Informational Text

The Healing Power of the Swiss Alps

Part 1 of 2

The Swiss Alps, with their majestic peaks, lush meadows, and crisp, clean air, have long been known for their restorative properties. In Heidi's story, we see how the natural beauty and tranquility of the mountains have a profound effect on her health and well-being. As Heidi adjusts to her new life in the Alps, she begins to thrive, her strength and vitality returning to her.

Page 12

The Healing Power of the Swiss Alps

Part 2 of 2

The close-knit family that Heidi finds in her grandfather and the local villagers also plays a crucial role in her healing. Their warmth, acceptance, and care provide Heidi with a sense of belonging that she had been missing. Together, the combination of the healing power of nature and the support of a loving community help Heidi to overcome the challenges she faced before arriving in the Swiss Alps.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. How does the natural environment of the Swiss Alps contribute to Heidi's healing?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. What role does Heidi's newfound family and community play in her recovery?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What are some specific examples from the story that demonstrate the healing power of nature and family?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Past Progressive Tense

What is it?
Adjectives are words that describe nouns. They help us better understand the characteristics of the people, places, and things in the story.

Example from today's reading:
"In the book, Heidi's grandfather is described as a 'stern' and 'gruff' man, but he also has a 'kind' and 'caring' side."

Your Turn:

Write three sentences using the past progressive tense to describe things happening in today's chapter.

Page 17

Informative Writing

Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

Remember: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Similes and Metaphors

Learn:
A simile compares two things using 'like' or 'as'. A metaphor says one thing IS another thing.

Activity:

Find the simile in today's reading or create your own simile about the story.

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 101!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 101! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Secret Garden.

Page 5 of 5

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What did Heidi do when she woke up on her first morning in the mountains?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

Why did Heidi feel so happy and content living in the mountain hut with her grandfather?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

How do you think Heidi's life in the mountains is different from her life in the city?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 12]

Page 11

Informational Text

The Healing Power of the Swiss Alps

Part 1 of 2

The Swiss Alps are a majestic mountain range known for their breathtaking landscapes, crisp alpine air, and healing properties. In the story of Heidi, the young girl is sent to live with her grandfather in the Swiss Alps, where she finds solace and rejuvenation in the natural world. The pure, untouched environment of the mountains has a profound effect on Heidi's well-being, both physically and emotionally.

Page 12

The Healing Power of the Swiss Alps

Part 2 of 2

As Heidi explores the rolling meadows, climbs the rugged peaks, and breathes in the fresh mountain air, she begins to heal from the hardships she has faced. The tranquility and beauty of the Swiss Alps provide a sense of peace and comfort that Heidi has never experienced before. Through her interactions with nature and her loving grandfather, Heidi learns the true value of family, community, and the restorative power of the natural world.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. How does the Swiss Alps environment help Heidi to heal?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. What aspects of the natural world in the Swiss Alps have a positive impact on Heidi?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What lessons does Heidi learn from living in the Swiss Alps?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Prepositional Phrases

What is it?
Adjectives are words that describe nouns or pronouns. They provide more information about the characteristics, qualities, or attributes of the thing being described.

Example from today's reading:
"In the story, Heidi encounters 'majestic mountains,' 'crisp alpine air,' and 'rolling meadows' in the Swiss Alps. These adjectives help us visualize and better understand the beautiful and restorative environment that Heidi experiences."

Your Turn:

Find three prepositional phrases in today's reading and circle the preposition in each one.

Page 17

Narrative Writing

Prompt:

Imagine you are Aunt Em in the cellar. Write about what you're thinking as the storm passes overhead.

Remember: Write a short story or scene with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Include descriptive details and dialogue if appropriate.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Context Clues

Learn:
Use surrounding words to figure out the meaning of an unfamiliar word.

Activity:

Find an unfamiliar word in the text and use context clues to determine its meaning before looking it up.

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 102!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 102! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Secret Garden.

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

Why did Heidi have to stay in the goatherd's hut instead of going back to the village?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

How did Heidi feel about having to stay in the hut instead of going back to the village?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

Do you think Heidi was right to insist on staying in the hut with the goatherd? Why or why not?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 13]

Page 11

Informational Text

The Beauty and Healing of the Swiss Alps

Part 1 of 2

The Swiss Alps are a majestic mountain range that have long been a source of wonder and inspiration. With towering peaks, lush meadows, and crystal-clear streams, the Alps offer a natural haven for both body and soul. In the story of Heidi, we see how the young girl's time in the Alps helps to heal and rejuvenate her. The fresh air, the simple living, and the close connection to nature all work together to restore Heidi's health and spirit.

Page 12

The Beauty and Healing of the Swiss Alps

Part 2 of 2

As Heidi explores the rugged terrain and spends her days caring for the goats, she discovers a deep appreciation for the beauty and power of the natural world. The Alps become a place of refuge and renewal, not just for Heidi but for her grandfather as well. Through their experiences in this magical landscape, the characters in the story learn valuable lessons about the importance of family, the healing power of nature, and the joy of living a simple, authentic life.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. How does the setting of the Swiss Alps contribute to the themes of the story?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. What are some of the ways that nature helps to heal and rejuvenate the characters in the story?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. How does Heidi's relationship with her grandfather change as a result of their time in the Alps?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Compound Sentences

What is it?
Adjectives are words that describe nouns, providing more detail and information about the subject. In Heidi, the author uses vivid adjectives to paint a picture of the Swiss Alps and the characters' experiences.

Example from today's reading:
"The author describes the 'towering peaks' and 'crystal-clear streams' of the Swiss Alps, using adjectives to create a rich, evocative image for the reader."

Your Turn:

Write three compound sentences about today's chapter using different coordinating conjunctions.

Page 17

Opinion Writing

Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

Remember: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Root Words and Affixes

Learn:
Many words are built from roots (base words) with prefixes (before) and suffixes (after) added.

Activity:

Break down vocabulary words into their parts: prefix + root + suffix. Example: uncomfortable = un + comfort + able

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 103!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 103! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Secret Garden.

Page 5 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Indian in the Cupboard - Chapter 14, Part 5

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What did Heidi do to help Grandfather on the mountain?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

Why did Heidi want to teach Peter to read?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

What do you think was the most important lesson Heidi learned while living with Grandfather on the mountain?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 14]

Page 11

Informational Text

Exploring the Healing Power of the Swiss Alps in Heidi

Part 1 of 2

In Johanna Spyri's classic novel 'Heidi,' the Swiss Alps serve as a breathtaking backdrop for the story's themes of nature, family, and healing. As Heidi settles into her new home with her grandfather in the mountains, she discovers the restorative power of the natural world, from the fresh alpine air to the lush meadows and towering peaks.

Page 12

Exploring the Healing Power of the Swiss Alps in Heidi

Part 2 of 2

Through Heidi's experiences, readers are transported to the rugged beauty of the Swiss landscape and witness how the tranquility and simplicity of life in the Alps can have a profound impact on one's physical and emotional well-being. The novel highlights the importance of connection to the natural environment and the healing that can occur within the embrace of a loving family.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. How does the setting of the Swiss Alps contribute to the themes of nature, family, and healing in 'Heidi'?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. What specific aspects of the natural world in the Alps help to restore Heidi and those around her?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. In what ways does Heidi's relationship with her grandfather and the local community reflect the importance of family and community in the novel?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Action vs. Linking Verbs

What is it?
In 'Heidi,' Johanna Spyri uses vivid descriptive language to paint a detailed picture of the Swiss Alps and the natural world. This helps readers immerse themselves in the story and better understand the importance of the setting.

Example from today's reading:
"Spyri describes the Alps as 'towering peaks' and the meadows as 'lush,' evoking a sense of grandeur and beauty in the natural environment."

Your Turn:

Find five verbs in today's reading and label each as action or linking.

Page 17

Informative Writing

Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

Remember: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Idioms and Figurative Language

Learn:
Idioms are phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say (like 'raining cats and dogs').

Activity:

Identify any idioms or figurative language in today's chapter and explain what they really mean.

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 104!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 104! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Secret Garden.

  • Read from The Railway Children
  • Learn 2 new vocabulary words
  • Read informational text: "Related Non-Fiction"
  • Practice grammar: Past Progressive Tense
  • Write: Opinion Writing
  • Explore: Similes and Metaphors

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

Why did Heidi's grandfather take her to the mountain pasture?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

How did Heidi's grandfather's behavior towards her change after she returned from the city?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

Do you think Heidi was happy living on the mountain with her grandfather? Explain your answer.

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 16]

Page 11

Informational Text

Exploring the Majestic Swiss Alps: Heidi's Healing Adventure

Part 1 of 2

Nestled in the heart of Europe, the Swiss Alps are a breathtaking natural wonder. Towering peaks, lush meadows, and crystal-clear streams create a serene and picturesque landscape that has captivated the imagination of people for centuries. In Heidi's story, the Swiss Alps play a crucial role in the young girl's journey, as she discovers the healing power of nature and the importance of family.

Page 12

Exploring the Majestic Swiss Alps: Heidi's Healing Adventure

Part 2 of 2

As Heidi explores the mountains, she finds solace in the simple pleasures of life, such as climbing the rocky paths, breathing in the crisp mountain air, and watching the goats graze on the lush vegetation. The tranquility of the Alps helps to soothe Heidi's spirit and provides her with a sense of belonging that she had been missing in the city.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. How do the Swiss Alps contribute to Heidi's healing journey?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. What are some of the natural wonders that Heidi discovers in the mountains?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. Why is the setting of the Swiss Alps important to the overall themes of the story?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Past Progressive Tense

What is it?
Adjectives are words that describe nouns or pronouns. In this chapter, the author uses vivid adjectives to paint a picture of the Swiss Alps and Heidi's experiences.

Example from today's reading:
"The author describes the 'towering peaks' and 'crystal-clear streams' of the Swiss Alps, helping the reader to visualize the majestic landscape."

Your Turn:

Write three sentences using the past progressive tense to describe things happening in today's chapter.

Page 17

Opinion Writing

Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

Remember: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Similes and Metaphors

Learn:
A simile compares two things using 'like' or 'as'. A metaphor says one thing IS another thing.

Activity:

Find the simile in today's reading or create your own simile about the story.

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 106!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 106! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Secret Garden.

  • Write: Informative Writing
  • Explore: Context Clues

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

Page 5 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Indian in the Cupboard - Chapter 17, Part 5

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

Why did Heidi want to go back to the mountains?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

How do you think Heidi felt when she saw the goats and the mountains again?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

If you were Heidi, would you have wanted to stay in the city or go back to the mountains? Why?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 17]

Page 11

Informational Text

The Majestic Swiss Alps: A Natural Wonderland

Part 1 of 2

Nestled in the heart of Europe, the Swiss Alps are a breathtaking natural wonder that have captivated the imaginations of people around the world. These towering peaks, capped with glistening snow, are home to a diverse array of plant and animal life, each adapted to the rugged terrain. The Alpine meadows are a riot of color, with wildflowers blooming in the warm summer sun, while the dense forests provide a sanctuary for elusive wildlife.

Page 12

The Majestic Swiss Alps: A Natural Wonderland

Part 2 of 2

For the people who call the Swiss Alps home, this stunning landscape is not only a source of beauty, but also a way of life. Generations of families have lived in the small villages that dot the mountainsides, passing down traditional skills and knowledge that have allowed them to thrive in this harsh but beautiful environment. The sense of community and connection to the land is a powerful force that has helped to heal and sustain these communities over the centuries.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. What makes the Swiss Alps a natural wonder?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. How do the people who live in the Swiss Alps connect to the land?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What role does the natural environment play in the lives of the people who live in the Swiss Alps?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Prepositional Phrases

What is it?
Adverbs of frequency, such as 'always', 'usually', and 'rarely', are used to describe how often an action occurs. These adverbs can provide important context about the characters' habits and routines.

Example from today's reading:
"In the story, Heidi 'always' climbed the mountain to visit her grandfather, demonstrating her strong connection to the natural world."

Your Turn:

Find three prepositional phrases in today's reading and circle the preposition in each one.

Page 17

Informative Writing

Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

Remember: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Context Clues

Learn:
Use surrounding words to figure out the meaning of an unfamiliar word.

Activity:

Find an unfamiliar word in the text and use context clues to determine its meaning before looking it up.

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 107!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 107! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Secret Garden.

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What did Heidi and her grandfather do when they first woke up on the mountain?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

Why did Heidi's grandfather decide to take her down to the village?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

How do you think Heidi felt when she returned to the village after being on the mountain for so long?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 18]

Page 11

Informational Text

The Beauty of the Swiss Alps: A Nature Lover's Paradise

Part 1 of 2

The Swiss Alps are a breathtaking mountain range that stretches across the heart of Europe. With their towering peaks, lush green meadows, and crystal-clear streams, the Alps have long been a source of inspiration and wonder. In the classic novel "Heidi," the young protagonist finds solace and healing in the embrace of this natural wonderland, where she forms a deep connection with the land and the people who call it home.

Page 12

The Beauty of the Swiss Alps: A Nature Lover's Paradise

Part 2 of 2

As Heidi explores the rugged terrain, she discovers the power of the natural world to restore the body and soothe the soul. The fresh mountain air, the gentle rustling of the trees, and the peaceful rhythm of the animals all contribute to a sense of tranquility and belonging that Heidi had never known before. Through her experiences in the Swiss Alps, Heidi learns the importance of family, community, and the healing properties of the great outdoors.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. What are some of the key features of the Swiss Alps that are described in the book?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. How does Heidi's connection to the natural world contribute to her overall well-being?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What role does the Swiss Alps play in Heidi's personal growth and development?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Compound Sentences

What is it?
In this chapter, the author uses a variety of descriptive adjectives to paint a vivid picture of the Swiss Alps. Students can practice identifying and using adjectives to describe the natural setting and its features.

Example from today's reading:
"The majestic, snow-capped peaks of the Swiss Alps towered over the lush, green meadows."

Your Turn:

Write three compound sentences about today's chapter using different coordinating conjunctions.

Page 17

Narrative Writing

Prompt:

Imagine you are Aunt Em in the cellar. Write about what you're thinking as the storm passes overhead.

Remember: Write a short story or scene with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Include descriptive details and dialogue if appropriate.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Root Words and Affixes

Learn:
Many words are built from roots (base words) with prefixes (before) and suffixes (after) added.

Activity:

Break down vocabulary words into their parts: prefix + root + suffix. Example: uncomfortable = un + comfort + able

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 108!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 108! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Secret Garden.

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

How did Heidi feel when she had to leave the mountains and go live with her aunt in the city?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

Why do you think Heidi's grandfather was so upset when he had to say goodbye to Heidi?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

Do you think Heidi will be happy living in the city with her aunt? Why or why not?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 19]

Page 11

Informational Text

The Beauty and Tranquility of the Swiss Alps

Part 1 of 2

The Swiss Alps are a breathtaking mountain range that have captivated the hearts and minds of people for centuries. With towering peaks, lush valleys, and crystal-clear streams, the Alps offer a serene and natural escape from the hustle and bustle of everyday life. In the story of Heidi, the young protagonist finds solace and healing in the embrace of this magnificent landscape, surrounded by the love and support of her family.

Page 12

The Beauty and Tranquility of the Swiss Alps

Part 2 of 2

As Heidi explores the rugged terrain, she discovers the power of nature to soothe the soul and restore the spirit. The fresh mountain air, the sound of the rushing water, and the sight of the majestic peaks all contribute to Heidi's sense of peace and well-being. Through her experiences in the Alps, Heidi learns valuable lessons about the importance of family, the healing power of the natural world, and the resilience of the human spirit.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. What are some of the key features of the Swiss Alps that are described in the story of Heidi?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. How does the natural environment of the Swiss Alps contribute to Heidi's sense of healing and well-being?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What role does Heidi's family play in her experiences in the Swiss Alps?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Action vs. Linking Verbs

What is it?
Adjectives are words that describe nouns or pronouns. They provide more detail and information about the subject.

Example from today's reading:
"In the story, Heidi lives in the 'majestic' Swiss Alps and enjoys the 'serene' and 'tranquil' natural environment."

Your Turn:

Find five verbs in today's reading and label each as action or linking.

Page 17

Opinion Writing

Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

Remember: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Idioms and Figurative Language

Learn:
Idioms are phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say (like 'raining cats and dogs').

Activity:

Identify any idioms or figurative language in today's chapter and explain what they really mean.

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 109!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 109! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Secret Garden.

Day 110: Day 110 Assessment - 3rd Grade BedrockELA

Page 1

Lesson 110

Assessment Day

The Secret Garden

Page 2

Assessment Instructions

Today is an assessment day! You'll be tested on:

  • Vocabulary: 10 words from Days 106-110
  • Reading Comprehension: Questions about the chapters
  • Grammar: Adjectives and Adjective Order
  • Writing: Informative Writing

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

Tip: Take your time and do your best work!

Page 3

Vocabulary Assessment

Write the definition for each word. Use complete sentences.

1. delightful

2. humble

3. radiant

4. miracle

5. terrific

6. solemn

7. earnest

8. whisper

9. struggle

10. comfort

Page 4

Reading Comprehension Test

Answer these questions about the chapters you read this week:

Question 1:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 2:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 3:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 4:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 5:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Page 5

Grammar Assessment

Adjectives and Adjective Order

Write three sentences with multiple adjectives describing characters or places from the chapter.

Page 6

Writing Assessment

Informative Writing

Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

Requirements: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

Page 7

Assessment Complete!

Great work on your assessment! You've completed 5 full days of The Secret Garden.

See you on Day 111!

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What did Heidi do to make the goats come to her?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

Why did Heidi feel sad when Grandfather said they would be going back to the village?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

What do you think Heidi learned about herself by taking care of the goats on the mountain?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 21]

Page 11

Informational Text

Exploring the Magical Swiss Alps

Part 1 of 2

The Swiss Alps are a breathtaking mountain range that stretches across the heart of Europe. With towering peaks, verdant meadows, and crystal-clear streams, this region is a true natural wonder. Heidi and her grandfather live in a cozy cottage nestled among the mountains, surrounded by the beauty of the alpine landscape. The fresh mountain air and the peaceful solitude of their home have a healing effect on both of them, as they find comfort in the embrace of the natural world.

Page 12

Exploring the Magical Swiss Alps

Part 2 of 2

The Swiss Alps are more than just a stunning backdrop for Heidi's story. They represent a connection to the land, to family, and to the simple joys of life. As Heidi explores the mountains and meadows, she discovers a sense of belonging and a deep appreciation for the beauty that surrounds her. The healing power of nature and the love of her grandfather help Heidi overcome the challenges she faces, reminding us all of the importance of finding solace in the natural world.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. How does the natural beauty of the Swiss Alps contribute to Heidi's healing and well-being?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. What role does Heidi's grandfather play in helping her connect with the natural world?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What specific examples from the story demonstrate the healing power of the alpine landscape?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Past Progressive Tense

What is it?
Adjectives are words that describe nouns. In this chapter, the author uses many descriptive adjectives to paint a vivid picture of the Swiss Alps and Heidi's surroundings.

Example from today's reading:
"The author describes the 'towering peaks,' 'verdant meadows,' and 'crystal-clear streams' of the Swiss Alps, using adjectives to convey the grandeur and beauty of Heidi's home."

Your Turn:

Write three sentences using the past progressive tense to describe things happening in today's chapter.

Page 17

Narrative Writing

Prompt:

Imagine you are Aunt Em in the cellar. Write about what you're thinking as the storm passes overhead.

Remember: Write a short story or scene with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Include descriptive details and dialogue if appropriate.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Similes and Metaphors

Learn:
A simile compares two things using 'like' or 'as'. A metaphor says one thing IS another thing.

Activity:

Find the simile in today's reading or create your own simile about the story.

Excellent Work!

Question 3:

Do you think Heidi enjoyed living on the mountain more than living in the city? Explain your answer.

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 22]

Page 11

Informational Text

The Beauty and Healing Powers of the Swiss Alps

Part 1 of 2

The Swiss Alps are a breathtaking mountain range that have long captivated the hearts and minds of people around the world. With towering peaks, lush green valleys, and crystal-clear streams, this natural wonder is a true testament to the power and beauty of the natural world. For Heidi, the young protagonist of the beloved novel, the Swiss Alps are not just a beautiful backdrop, but a place of healing and belonging.

Page 12

The Beauty and Healing Powers of the Swiss Alps

Part 2 of 2

As Heidi settles into her new home high in the mountains, she begins to experience the restorative effects of the fresh air, the gentle breezes, and the simple, but deeply fulfilling life in the Alps. The close-knit family that takes her in, the grandfather who finds solace in her presence, and the vast, untamed nature that surrounds her all contribute to Heidi's physical and emotional healing. The Swiss Alps become a sanctuary where she can truly thrive and grow.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. How do the Swiss Alps contribute to Heidi's healing and well-being?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. What aspects of the natural world in the Swiss Alps help Heidi to feel at home and content?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. How does the close-knit family that takes Heidi in support her emotional and physical healing?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Prepositional Phrases

What is it?
Compound sentences are made up of two independent clauses joined by a coordinating conjunction (such as 'and', 'but', or 'or').

Example from today's reading:
"Heidi loved the fresh air of the Swiss Alps, and she felt her spirits lift with each passing day."

Your Turn:

Find three prepositional phrases in today's reading and circle the preposition in each one.

Page 17

Opinion Writing

Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

Remember: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Context Clues

Learn:
Use surrounding words to figure out the meaning of an unfamiliar word.

Activity:

Find an unfamiliar word in the text and use context clues to determine its meaning before looking it up.

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 112!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 112! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Secret Garden.

📚 Placeholder for The Indian in the Cupboard - Chapter 23, Part 5

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What did Heidi do to help Grandfather when he was feeling sad?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

Why do you think Heidi's visit brought joy to Grandfather?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

What do you think Heidi learned about her Grandfather's character during this chapter?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 23]

Page 11

Informational Text

Exploring the Majestic Swiss Alps

Part 1 of 2

The Swiss Alps are a breathtaking mountain range that stretches across the heart of Europe. This incredible landscape is home to towering peaks, pristine glaciers, and lush alpine meadows. The people who live in the Swiss Alps have a deep connection to the natural world, and they have learned to thrive in this rugged environment.

Page 12

Exploring the Majestic Swiss Alps

Part 2 of 2

Heidi, the young protagonist of our story, has been raised in the Swiss Alps, and she finds comfort and healing in the beauty of her surroundings. The fresh air, the gentle streams, and the towering mountains all contribute to Heidi's sense of peace and well-being. As she spends time with her grandfather and her new friends, Heidi's appreciation for the natural world only grows stronger.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. What are some of the key features of the Swiss Alps described in the article?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. How does Heidi's connection to the natural world contribute to her sense of healing and well-being?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What are some ways that the people of the Swiss Alps have learned to thrive in their environment?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Compound Sentences

What is it?
Adjectives are words that describe nouns or pronouns. In this chapter of Heidi, the author uses many descriptive adjectives to paint a vivid picture of the Swiss Alps and Heidi's surroundings.

Example from today's reading:
"For example, the author describes the 'towering peaks', 'pristine glaciers', and 'lush alpine meadows' of the Swiss Alps, using adjectives to give the reader a more vivid and immersive experience."

Your Turn:

Write three compound sentences about today's chapter using different coordinating conjunctions.

Page 17

Informative Writing

Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

Remember: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Root Words and Affixes

Learn:
Many words are built from roots (base words) with prefixes (before) and suffixes (after) added.

Activity:

Break down vocabulary words into their parts: prefix + root + suffix. Example: uncomfortable = un + comfort + able

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 113!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 113! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Secret Garden.

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What did Heidi and Peter do when they went up to the mountain on their own?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

Why do you think Heidi was so happy to be back on the mountain with her grandfather?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

How do you think Heidi's life on the mountain is different from her life in the city with Clara?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 24]

Page 11

Informational Text

The Beauty of the Swiss Alps

Part 1 of 2

The Swiss Alps are a stunning mountain range that stretch across the heart of Europe. With towering peaks, lush alpine meadows, and crystal-clear lakes, the Alps are a true natural wonder. In the story of Heidi, the young girl is sent to live with her grandfather in the Swiss Alps, where she discovers the beauty and healing power of the natural world.

Page 12

The Beauty of the Swiss Alps

Part 2 of 2

As Heidi explores the mountains and valleys, she learns to appreciate the simple joys of life, such as the warmth of the sun, the gentle breeze, and the song of the birds. The fresh air and peaceful surroundings help to heal her own spirit, as well as that of her grandfather, who has become withdrawn and isolated in his mountain home.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. What are some of the key features of the Swiss Alps that are described in the story?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. How does the natural setting of the Swiss Alps help to heal Heidi and her grandfather?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What are some of the ways that Heidi learns to appreciate the beauty and power of the natural world?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Action vs. Linking Verbs

What is it?
Compound sentences are made up of two or more independent clauses joined by a conjunction, such as 'and', 'but', or 'or'.

Example from today's reading:
"In the book, Heidi says, 'The goats are my friends, and I love to watch them play in the meadow.'"

Your Turn:

Find five verbs in today's reading and label each as action or linking.

Page 17

Narrative Writing

Prompt:

Imagine you are Aunt Em in the cellar. Write about what you're thinking as the storm passes overhead.

Remember: Write a short story or scene with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Include descriptive details and dialogue if appropriate.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Idioms and Figurative Language

Learn:
Idioms are phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say (like 'raining cats and dogs').

Activity:

Identify any idioms or figurative language in today's chapter and explain what they really mean.

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 114!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 114! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Secret Garden.

Day 115: Day 115 Assessment - 3rd Grade BedrockELA

Page 1

Lesson 115

Assessment Day

The Secret Garden

Page 2

Assessment Instructions

Today is an assessment day! You'll be tested on:

  • Vocabulary: 10 words from Days 111-115
  • Reading Comprehension: Questions about the chapters
  • Grammar: Adjectives and Adjective Order
  • Writing: Opinion Writing

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

Tip: Take your time and do your best work!

Page 3

Vocabulary Assessment

Write the definition for each word. Use complete sentences.

1. delightful

2. humble

3. radiant

4. miracle

5. terrific

6. solemn

7. earnest

8. whisper

9. struggle

10. comfort

Page 4

Question 1:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 3:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 4:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 5:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Page 5

Grammar Assessment

Adjectives and Adjective Order

Write three sentences with multiple adjectives describing characters or places from the chapter.

Page 6

Writing Assessment

Opinion Writing

Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

Requirements: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

Page 7

Assessment Complete!

Great work on your assessment! You've completed 5 full days of The Secret Garden.

See you on Day 116!

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What did Heidi do when she first arrived at the Alm-Uncle's hut?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

Why do you think Heidi felt so happy and content living on the mountain with the Alm-Uncle?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

What do you think Heidi learned about herself and the world while living on the mountain?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 26]

Page 11

Informational Text

The Majestic Swiss Alps: A Sanctuary for Healing and Family

Part 1 of 2

Nestled high in the heart of Europe, the Swiss Alps stand as a breathtaking natural wonder, a haven for those seeking solace and renewal. In this captivating region, the story of Heidi unfolds, where the young girl's journey connects her with the rugged, yet serene, landscape. The towering peaks, verdant meadows, and rushing streams become not only a backdrop but a character in their own right, offering a sense of peace and restoration to those who venture within.

Page 12

The Majestic Swiss Alps: A Sanctuary for Healing and Family

Part 2 of 2

As Heidi embraces the beauty of her Alpine home, she discovers the true meaning of family and the healing power of the natural world. The simple yet profound lessons she learns from her grandfather and the villagers serve as a testament to the rejuvenating effects of a life in harmony with nature. Through Heidi's eyes, readers are invited to explore the wonders of the Swiss Alps and the profound impact they can have on the human spirit.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. How does the Swiss Alps landscape serve as a character in the story of Heidi?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. What lessons does Heidi learn from her life in the mountains that contribute to her personal growth and healing?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. How do the themes of nature, family, and healing interconnect in the story of Heidi?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Past Progressive Tense

What is it?
Adjectives are used to describe nouns and provide more detailed information. In this chapter of Heidi, the author uses vivid adjectives to paint a rich picture of the Swiss Alps and its surrounding environment.

Example from today's reading:
"Examples of adjectives used in the book include 'majestic', 'rugged', 'serene', and 'verdant' when describing the Swiss Alps."

Your Turn:

Write three sentences using the past progressive tense to describe things happening in today's chapter.

Page 17

Informative Writing

Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

Remember: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Similes and Metaphors

Learn:
A simile compares two things using 'like' or 'as'. A metaphor says one thing IS another thing.

Activity:

Find the simile in today's reading or create your own simile about the story.

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 116!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 116! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Secret Garden.

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 27]

Page 11

Informational Text

The Beauty of the Swiss Alps

Part 1 of 2

The Swiss Alps are a breathtaking mountain range that have captivated the hearts of people for centuries. From the towering peaks to the lush, green valleys, the natural beauty of this region is truly awe-inspiring. Heidi, the young protagonist of the story, is fortunate to call the Swiss Alps her home, where she can freely explore the wonders of nature and spend time with her beloved grandfather.

Page 12

The Beauty of the Swiss Alps

Part 2 of 2

As Heidi explores the Swiss Alps, she discovers the healing power of the natural world. The fresh air, the tranquil streams, and the warm sunshine all work together to soothe her soul and help her find a sense of peace. The strong bond between Heidi and her grandfather, who has lived in the Swiss Alps for many years, further emphasizes the importance of family and community in this enchanting story.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. What are some of the natural features of the Swiss Alps described in the story?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. How does Heidi's exploration of the Swiss Alps help her find healing and peace?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. How does the relationship between Heidi and her grandfather contribute to the themes of family and community in the story?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Prepositional Phrases

What is it?
Adjectives are words that describe nouns. In this chapter, the author uses many descriptive adjectives to paint a vivid picture of the Swiss Alps.

Example from today's reading:
"The author describes the 'towering peaks' and 'lush, green valleys' of the Swiss Alps, using adjectives to convey the grandeur and beauty of the natural landscape."

Your Turn:

Find three prepositional phrases in today's reading and circle the preposition in each one.

Page 17

Narrative Writing

Prompt:

Imagine you are Aunt Em in the cellar. Write about what you're thinking as the storm passes overhead.

Remember: Write a short story or scene with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Include descriptive details and dialogue if appropriate.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Context Clues

Learn:
Use surrounding words to figure out the meaning of an unfamiliar word.

Find an unfamiliar word in the text and use context clues to determine its meaning before looking it up.

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What did Heidi do to help Grandfather when he was sick?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

Why do you think Heidi was so excited to see the snow for the first time?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

Do you think Heidi's life in the mountains was better or worse than her life in the city? Explain your answer.

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 28]

Page 11

Informational Text

The Healing Power of the Swiss Alps

Part 1 of 2

The Swiss Alps are a stunning mountain range that have long been known for their ability to heal and rejuvenate the spirit. With their towering peaks, lush green meadows, and crystal-clear streams, the Alps provide a serene and peaceful environment that can have a profound effect on the mind and body. In the story of Heidi, we see how the young girl is able to thrive in this natural setting, surrounded by the love and care of her grandfather and the animals of the mountain.

Page 12

The Healing Power of the Swiss Alps

Part 2 of 2

As Heidi spends time in the Alps, her health and happiness begin to blossom. The fresh air, the exercise of climbing the mountains, and the connection to the natural world all contribute to her physical and emotional healing. This is a testament to the power of the Swiss Alps to provide a restorative and nurturing environment for those in need of healing, whether it be from illness, trauma, or simply the stresses of everyday life.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. What are some of the ways that the Swiss Alps are described as being able to heal and rejuvenate people?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. How does Heidi's experience in the Alps contribute to her own healing and growth?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What are some of the specific elements of the natural environment that are said to have a healing effect?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Compound Sentences

What is it?
Adjectives are words that describe nouns. In this chapter, the author uses many adjectives to paint a vivid picture of the Swiss Alps and Heidi's experiences there.

Example from today's reading:
"Some examples of adjectives used in the story include 'towering', 'lush', 'crystal-clear', 'serene', and 'peaceful'."

Your Turn:

Write three compound sentences about today's chapter using different coordinating conjunctions.

Page 17

Opinion Writing

Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

Remember: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Root Words and Affixes

Learn:
Many words are built from roots (base words) with prefixes (before) and suffixes (after) added.

Activity:

Break down vocabulary words into their parts: prefix + root + suffix. Example: uncomfortable = un + comfort + able

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 118!

Great Job!

Reading: The Indian in the Cupboard

Book: The Railway Children by E. Nesbit

📚 Placeholder for The Indian in the Cupboard - Chapter 29, Part 2

Page 3 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Indian in the Cupboard - Chapter 29, Part 3

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 4 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Indian in the Cupboard - Chapter 29, Part 4

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 8

Page 5 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Indian in the Cupboard - Chapter 29, Part 5

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What chore did Heidi have to do every day in the mountains?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

Why did Heidi feel sad when she had to leave the mountain and go to Frankfurt?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

Do you think Heidi should have been allowed to stay in the mountains instead of going to Frankfurt? Why or why not?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 29]

Page 11

Informational Text

The Majestic Swiss Alps: A Healing Embrace

Part 1 of 2

The Swiss Alps, with their towering peaks and lush valleys, have long been a place of wonder and healing. Nestled among these grand mountains, the story of Heidi unfolds, showcasing the restorative power of nature and the bonds of family. Heidi's newfound home in the Alps provides her with a sense of belonging and the opportunity to thrive, as she explores the breathtaking landscapes and connects with the people around her.

Page 12

The Majestic Swiss Alps: A Healing Embrace

Part 2 of 2

The Alps not only serve as a backdrop to Heidi's story but also play a crucial role in her own healing journey. The fresh mountain air, the gentle streams, and the vast expanse of the natural world become a source of comfort and rejuvenation for Heidi, allowing her to overcome the challenges she has faced. Through her interactions with the land and its inhabitants, Heidi learns valuable lessons about the importance of family, community, and the power of embracing the natural world.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. How do the Swiss Alps contribute to Heidi's healing and personal growth?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. What are some of the specific aspects of the natural world that Heidi finds comforting and rejuvenating?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. How do the bonds of family and community play a role in Heidi's experiences in the Swiss Alps?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Action vs. Linking Verbs

What is it?
Adjective phrases are groups of words that modify a noun or pronoun. They provide more detailed information about the subject.

Example from today's reading:
"In the chapter, Heidi describes the 'towering peaks' and 'lush valleys' of the Swiss Alps, using adjective phrases to paint a vivid picture of the majestic landscape."

Your Turn:

Find five verbs in today's reading and label each as action or linking.

Page 17

Informative Writing

Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

Remember: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Idioms and Figurative Language

Learn:
Idioms are phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say (like 'raining cats and dogs').

Activity:

Identify any idioms or figurative language in today's chapter and explain what they really mean.

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 119!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 119! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Secret Garden.

Lesson 120

Assessment Day

The Secret Garden

Assessment Instructions

Today is an assessment day! You'll be tested on:

  • Vocabulary: 10 words from Days 116-120

  • Grammar: Adjectives and Adjective Order

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

Tip: Take your time and do your best work!

Page 3

Vocabulary Assessment

1. delightful

2. humble

3. radiant

4. miracle

5. terrific

6. solemn

7. earnest

8. whisper

9. struggle

10. comfort

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Page 5

Adjectives and Adjective Order

Page 6

Writing Assessment

Narrative Writing

Prompt:

Requirements: Write a short story or scene with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Include descriptive details and dialogue if appropriate.

Page 3

Reading: A Little Princess

Chapter 1

Book: A Little Princess by Frances Hodgson Burnett

Page 5

Page 2 of 5

Page 4 of 5

📚 Placeholder for A Little Princess - Chapter 1, Part 4

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 8

Page 5 of 5

📚 Placeholder for A Little Princess - Chapter 1, Part 5

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What was the name of the town where Dr. Dolittle lived?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

Why did the people in the town stop going to Dr. Dolittle for help?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

Do you think Dr. Dolittle was a good doctor for people? Why or why not?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 1]

Page 11

Informational Text

The Amazing World of Animals

Part 1 of 2

In the story of Dr. Dolittle, we learn about a very special man who can talk to animals. This is an amazing ability that not many people have. Animals are fascinating creatures, and they communicate in their own unique ways. From the chirping of birds to the growling of lions, the animal kingdom is full of diverse and interesting ways of communicating.

Page 12

The Amazing World of Animals

Part 2 of 2

As we follow Dr. Dolittle on his adventures, we'll discover the importance of understanding and respecting the animals around us. Whether it's helping a sick animal or going on a journey to a faraway land, communication is key to building a strong relationship between humans and the natural world.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. What special ability does Dr. Dolittle have?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. How do animals communicate in the story?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. Why is communication important in the relationship between humans and animals?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Past Progressive Tense

What is it?
Nouns are words that name people, places, things, or ideas. In the story of Dr. Dolittle, we'll find many different nouns that describe the animals and characters.

Example from today's reading:
"Some examples of nouns in the story include 'parrot', 'monkey', and 'Dr. Dolittle'."

Your Turn:

Write three sentences using the past progressive tense to describe things happening in today's chapter.

Page 17

Opinion Writing

Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

Remember: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Similes and Metaphors

Learn:
A simile compares two things using 'like' or 'as'. A metaphor says one thing IS another thing.

Activity:

Find the simile in today's reading or create your own simile about the story.

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 121!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 121! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Railway Children.

Page 5 of 5

📚 Placeholder for Pippi Longstocking - Chapter 2, Part 5

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What was the name of the town where Dr. Dolittle lived?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

Why did the people in the town stop going to Dr. Dolittle for help?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

Do you think it was fair for the people to stop going to Dr. Dolittle? Why or why not?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 2]

Page 11

Informational Text

Talking to the Animals

Part 1 of 2

Have you ever wondered what it would be like to talk to the animals? In the story of Dr. Dolittle, the main character can understand and speak to all kinds of animals. This is a special ability that not many people have. Being able to communicate with animals can lead to amazing adventures and help you learn a lot about the natural world.

Page 12

Talking to the Animals

Part 2 of 2

Imagine if you could ask a bird about its long journey south for the winter, or talk to a dolphin about life in the ocean. You might discover secrets about the animal kingdom that humans don't know. With the power of communication, Dr. Dolittle is able to help sick animals and go on exciting voyages around the world.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. What special ability does Dr. Dolittle have?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. How can being able to talk to animals lead to adventures?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What might you learn if you could communicate with animals?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Prepositional Phrases

What is it?
Nouns are words that name a person, place, thing, or idea. In this story, there are many nouns that name different animals and objects.

Example from today's reading:
"Some nouns from the story are 'bird', 'dolphin', 'ship', and 'doctor'."

Your Turn:

Find three prepositional phrases in today's reading and circle the preposition in each one.

Page 17

Informative Writing

Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

Remember: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Context Clues

Learn:
Use surrounding words to figure out the meaning of an unfamiliar word.

Activity:

Find an unfamiliar word in the text and use context clues to determine its meaning before looking it up.

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 122!

Great Job!

Page 5 of 5

📚 Placeholder for Pippi Longstocking - Chapter 3, Part 5

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What was the name of the town where Dr. Dolittle lived?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

Why did the people of the town stop going to Dr. Dolittle for help?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

Do you think Dr. Dolittle made the right decision to stop treating people and focus on animals? Why or why not?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 3]

Page 11

Informational Text

Talking to the Animals

Part 1 of 2

Have you ever wished you could talk to the animals like Dr. Dolittle? In the story, Dr. Dolittle is a kind and curious doctor who can speak to all kinds of animals. He learns their languages so he can help them and become their friend. Being able to communicate with animals opens up a whole new world of adventure and discovery.

Page 12

Talking to the Animals

Part 2 of 2

Imagine how amazing it would be to be able to ask a parrot for directions, or have a cat explain what it needs. With communication, we can learn so much from the animals around us. They can teach us about their homes, their families, and their needs. If we open our ears and our hearts, we might be surprised by the stories the animals have to share.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. How does Dr. Dolittle's ability to talk to animals help him in the story?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. What are some benefits of being able to communicate with animals?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What new things do you think you could learn if you could talk to the animals around you?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Compound Sentences

What is it?
Nouns are words that name people, places, things, or ideas. Pronouns are words that take the place of nouns, like 'he', 'she', 'it', or 'they'.

Example from today's reading:
"In the story, Dr. Dolittle talks to many different animals. He communicates with 'them' and learns about 'their' lives."

Your Turn:

Write three compound sentences about today's chapter using different coordinating conjunctions.

Page 17

Narrative Writing

Prompt:

Imagine you are Aunt Em in the cellar. Write about what you're thinking as the storm passes overhead.

Remember: Write a short story or scene with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Include descriptive details and dialogue if appropriate.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Root Words and Affixes

Learn:
Many words are built from roots (base words) with prefixes (before) and suffixes (after) added.

Activity:

Break down vocabulary words into their parts: prefix + root + suffix. Example: uncomfortable = un + comfort + able

Excellent Work!

Great Job!

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What was the name of the doctor in the story?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

Why did the doctor start to learn the language of animals?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

Do you think the doctor made the right choice to start learning animal languages? Why or why not?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 4]

Page 11

Informational Text

Animal Wonders: Communicating with Our Furry Friends

Part 1 of 2

Have you ever wondered what it would be like to talk to animals? In the story of Dr. Dolittle, the main character can understand and talk to all kinds of animals. This is a fascinating ability that allows him to go on amazing adventures and learn about the world around him. Animals have their own ways of communicating, using sounds, body language, and even special scents to convey their thoughts and feelings.

Page 12

Animal Wonders: Communicating with Our Furry Friends

Part 2 of 2

Imagine being able to ask a dog what it's thinking or have a conversation with a bird. Dr. Dolittle's special gift lets him do just that. Through his interactions with the animals, he learns about their needs, their behaviors, and their perspectives on the world. This understanding helps him find ways to help and protect the animals he meets, making him a true friend to all creatures great and small.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. What special ability does Dr. Dolittle have that allows him to go on adventures?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. How do animals communicate with each other?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. How does Dr. Dolittle's ability to understand animals help him in the story?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Action vs. Linking Verbs

What is it?
Adjectives are words that describe nouns. They can help us paint a more vivid picture of the people, places, and things in the story.

Example from today's reading:
"In the story, Dr. Dolittle has a kind and gentle way of communicating with the animals."

Your Turn:

Find five verbs in today's reading and label each as action or linking.

Page 17

Opinion Writing

Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

Remember: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Idioms and Figurative Language

Learn:
Idioms are phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say (like 'raining cats and dogs').

Activity:

Identify any idioms or figurative language in today's chapter and explain what they really mean.

Excellent Work!

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 7

Page 4 of 5

📚 Placeholder for Pippi Longstocking - Chapter 6, Part 4

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 8

Page 5 of 5

📚 Placeholder for Pippi Longstocking - Chapter 6, Part 5

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What was the name of the animal that Dr. Dolittle could talk to?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

Why did the doctor decide to learn how to talk to animals?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

Do you think it was a good idea for Dr. Dolittle to learn how to talk to animals? Why or why not?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 6]

Page 11

Informational Text

The Amazing World of Animal Communication

Part 1 of 2

Have you ever wondered how animals talk to each other? In the story of Dr. Dolittle, we learn that he has a special ability to understand and communicate with animals. This is because animals have their own unique ways of communicating, using a variety of sounds, body language, and even scents. From the chirping of birds to the roars of lions, the animal kingdom is full of fascinating conversations.

Page 12

The Amazing World of Animal Communication

Part 2 of 2

As Dr. Dolittle journeys through the story, he encounters all kinds of animals and learns about their different ways of communicating. He discovers that each species has its own language, and he is able to translate and understand them. This allows him to embark on amazing adventures and help the animals in need. By learning about animal communication, we can better appreciate the incredible diversity and intelligence of the creatures that share our world.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. What are some of the ways that animals communicate with each other?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. How does Dr. Dolittle's ability to understand animal communication help him in his adventures?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. Why is it important for us to learn about how animals communicate?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Past Progressive Tense

What is it?
Adjectives are words that describe nouns. They help us paint a clearer picture of the animals and characters in the story.

Example from today's reading:
"In the story, Dr. Dolittle is described as a kind and gentle doctor who cares deeply for the animals he encounters."

Your Turn:

Write three sentences using the past progressive tense to describe things happening in today's chapter.

Page 17

Narrative Writing

Prompt:

Imagine you are Aunt Em in the cellar. Write about what you're thinking as the storm passes overhead.

Remember: Write a short story or scene with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Include descriptive details and dialogue if appropriate.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Similes and Metaphors

Learn:
A simile compares two things using 'like' or 'as'. A metaphor says one thing IS another thing.

Activity:

Find the simile in today's reading or create your own simile about the story.

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 126!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 126! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Railway Children.

Lesson 127

3rd Grade ELA

The Railway Children

Chapter 7

Welcome to Lesson 127!

  • Read from A Little Princess
  • Learn 2 new vocabulary words
  • Read informational text: "Related Non-Fiction"
  • Practice grammar: Prepositional Phrases
  • Write: Opinion Writing

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

Page 4

Reading: Pippi Longstocking

Chapter 7

Page 1 of 5

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Book: A Little Princess by Frances Hodgson Burnett

Page 2 of 5

Page 3 of 5

📚 Placeholder for Pippi Longstocking - Chapter 7, Part 3

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 7

Page 4 of 5

📚 Placeholder for Pippi Longstocking - Chapter 7, Part 4

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 8

Page 5 of 5

📚 Placeholder for Pippi Longstocking - Chapter 7, Part 5

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What did the animals in Puddleby tell Dr. Dolittle they needed help with?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

Why do you think the animals came to Dr. Dolittle for help instead of going to other people?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

Do you think Dr. Dolittle was the right person to help the animals? Why or why not?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 7]

Page 11

Informational Text

The Power of Animal Communication

Part 1 of 2

Have you ever wondered how animals communicate with each other? In the story of Dr. Dolittle, the main character can talk to animals and understand their language. This allows him to go on exciting adventures and help the animals in need. Animals use a variety of sounds, body language, and even scents to communicate with one another.

Page 12

The Power of Animal Communication

Part 2 of 2

For example, dogs bark to warn others of danger or to show excitement. Cats meow to get attention or express their feelings. Birds sing to mark their territory and attract mates. Understanding how animals communicate is crucial for their survival and well-being. In the story, Dr. Dolittle's ability to talk to animals helps him solve problems and rescue them from dangerous situations.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. How do animals communicate with each other?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. What helps Dr. Dolittle go on adventures in the story?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. Why is understanding animal communication important?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Prepositional Phrases

What is it?
A compound sentence is made up of two independent clauses joined by a conjunction like 'and', 'but', or 'or'.

Example from today's reading:
"In the story, Dr. Dolittle could talk to the animals, and they could understand him."

Your Turn:

Find three prepositional phrases in today's reading and circle the preposition in each one.

Page 17

Opinion Writing

Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

Remember: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Context Clues

Learn:
Use surrounding words to figure out the meaning of an unfamiliar word.

Activity:

Find an unfamiliar word in the text and use context clues to determine its meaning before looking it up.

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 127!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 127! Come back tomorrow to continue reading The Railway Children.

Lesson 128

3rd Grade ELA

The Railway Children

Chapter 8

Welcome to Lesson 128!

Today you will:

  • Read from A Little Princess
  • Learn 2 new vocabulary words
  • Read informational text: "Related Non-Fiction"
  • Practice grammar: Compound Sentences
  • Write: Informative Writing
  • Explore: Root Words and Affixes

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

Page 2 of 5
