Page 1

Lesson 156

3rd Grade ELA

Heidi

Chapter 6

Page 2

Welcome to Lesson 156!

Today you will:

  • Read from The Secret Garden
  • Learn 2 new vocabulary words
  • Read informational text: "Related Non-Fiction"
  • Practice grammar: Past Progressive Tense
  • Write: Narrative Writing
  • Explore: Similes and Metaphors

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

Page 3

Vocabulary Words

corridor

Look up this word in a dictionary, then write the definition in your own words:

0 / 10 words
eerie

Look up this word in a dictionary, then write the definition in your own words:

0 / 10 words

Watch for these words in today's reading!

Page 4

Reading: The Secret Garden

Chapter 6

Page 1 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Secret Garden - Chapter 6, Part 1

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Book: The Secret Garden by Frances Hodgson Burnett

Page 5

Page 2 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Secret Garden - Chapter 6, Part 2

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 6

Page 3 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Secret Garden - Chapter 6, Part 3

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 7

Page 4 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Secret Garden - Chapter 6, Part 4

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 8

Page 5 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Secret Garden - Chapter 6, Part 5

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What did Robinson Crusoe do to make his cave more comfortable to live in?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

Why do you think Robinson Crusoe felt lonely and wished he had someone to talk to?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

Do you think Robinson Crusoe should have tried to escape the island sooner? Explain your answer.

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 6]

Page 11

Informational Text

Surviving on a Deserted Island

Part 1 of 2

Imagine being stranded on a remote island, far from civilization. This is exactly what happened to Robinson Crusoe, the main character in the classic novel by Daniel Defoe. Crusoe had to rely on his resourcefulness and determination to survive in this unfamiliar environment. He had to find food, shelter, and clean water, all while facing the challenges of island life.

Page 12

Surviving on a Deserted Island

Part 2 of 2

As Crusoe explored his new home, he discovered various natural resources that he could use to his advantage. He built a shelter, grew crops, and even domesticated some animals. Through his ingenuity and problem-solving skills, Crusoe was able to create a sustainable lifestyle on the island, showcasing the human capacity for resilience and survival.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. How did Robinson Crusoe's resourcefulness help him survive on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. What were some of the challenges Crusoe faced in his daily life on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What natural resources did Crusoe discover and use to meet his basic needs?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Past Progressive Tense

What is it?
Compound sentences are made up of two or more independent clauses joined by a coordinating conjunction (e.g., and, but, or).

Example from today's reading:
"In the book, Crusoe built a shelter, but he also grew crops to sustain himself."

Your Turn:

Write three sentences using the past progressive tense to describe things happening in today's chapter.

Page 17

Narrative Writing

Prompt:

Imagine you are Aunt Em in the cellar. Write about what you're thinking as the storm passes overhead.

Remember: Write a short story or scene with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Include descriptive details and dialogue if appropriate.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Similes and Metaphors

Learn:
A simile compares two things using 'like' or 'as'. A metaphor says one thing IS another thing.

Activity:

Find the simile in today's reading or create your own simile about the story.

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 156!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 156! Come back tomorrow to continue reading Heidi.

Reading: The Secret Garden

Page 8

Page 5 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Secret Garden - Chapter 7, Part 5

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What did Robinson Crusoe do to celebrate his 157th day on the island?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

How do you think Robinson Crusoe felt about being alone on the island for so long?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

Do you think Robinson Crusoe was brave for staying on the island instead of trying to escape? Why or why not?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 7]

Page 11

Informational Text

Surviving on a Deserted Island

Part 1 of 2

Imagine being stranded on a deserted island, with no one to help you and no way to get home. This is the situation that Robinson Crusoe found himself in. Despite being alone, he had to find ways to survive and make a life for himself on the island.

Page 12

Surviving on a Deserted Island

Part 2 of 2

Crusoe had to use his resourcefulness and problem-solving skills to build a shelter, find food and water, and protect himself from the elements. He had to learn how to hunt, grow crops, and make tools and other supplies from the materials he could find on the island.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. What are some of the challenges Crusoe faced while living on the deserted island?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. How did Crusoe use his resourcefulness to survive on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What lessons can we learn from Crusoe's story about surviving in difficult situations?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Prepositional Phrases

What is it?
Adjectives are words that describe nouns. In this story, Crusoe uses many adjectives to describe the island and his experiences.

Example from today's reading:
"For example, Crusoe might describe the island as 'desolate,' 'uninhabited,' or 'remote.'"

Your Turn:

Find three prepositional phrases in today's reading and circle the preposition in each one.

Page 17

Opinion Writing

Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

Remember: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Context Clues

Learn:
Use surrounding words to figure out the meaning of an unfamiliar word.

Activity:

Find an unfamiliar word in the text and use context clues to determine its meaning before looking it up.

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 157!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 157! Come back tomorrow to continue reading Heidi.

Page 8

Page 5 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Secret Garden - Chapter 8, Part 5

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What was Robinson Crusoe's job before he was shipwrecked on the island?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

Why do you think Robinson Crusoe decided to go on a voyage, even though his father advised him not to?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

How do you think Robinson Crusoe felt about being alone on the island for so long? Explain your answer.

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 8]

Page 11

Informational Text

Surviving on a Deserted Island

Part 1 of 2

Imagine being stranded on a deserted island, all alone with no one to help you. This is the exact situation that Robinson Crusoe found himself in. After his ship was wrecked, he had to rely on his own skills and resourcefulness to survive on the island. He had to find food, build shelter, and create tools to make his life more comfortable.

Page 12

Surviving on a Deserted Island

Part 2 of 2

Crusoe quickly realized that he would need to be very resourceful to make it on the island. He scavenged for materials, using everything he could find to create the necessary items he needed. From making clothes out of animal skins to building a makeshift home, Crusoe showed incredible ingenuity and determination to make the best of his situation.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. What were some of the challenges Crusoe faced on the deserted island?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. How did Crusoe use his resourcefulness to survive?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What items did Crusoe create using the materials he found on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Compound Sentences

What is it?
Compound sentences are made up of two independent clauses joined by a conjunction, such as 'and', 'but', or 'or'.

Example from today's reading:
"Crusoe built a shelter, and he also planted crops to have a reliable food source."

Your Turn:

Write three compound sentences about today's chapter using different coordinating conjunctions.

Page 17

Informative Writing

Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

Remember: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Root Words and Affixes

Learn:
Many words are built from roots (base words) with prefixes (before) and suffixes (after) added.

Activity:

Break down vocabulary words into their parts: prefix + root + suffix. Example: uncomfortable = un + comfort + able

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 158!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 158! Come back tomorrow to continue reading Heidi.

Page 5 of 5

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What happened to Robinson Crusoe's ship during the storm?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

Why do you think Crusoe was the only survivor of the shipwreck?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

Do you think Crusoe made the right decision to stay on the island instead of trying to find help? Explain your answer.

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 9]

Page 11

Informational Text

How Robinson Crusoe Survived on the Island

Part 1 of 2

When Robinson Crusoe was shipwrecked on the island, he had to learn how to survive on his own. He had to find food, water, and shelter to stay alive. Crusoe was very resourceful and used the items he found on the island to build a home and tools.

Page 12

How Robinson Crusoe Survived on the Island

Part 2 of 2

Crusoe also had to be careful of wild animals on the island. He had to find ways to protect himself and his home. Despite the challenges, Crusoe was able to live on the island for many years and even grow his own food. His story shows us how important it is to be resourceful and adaptable when facing difficult situations.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. What did Robinson Crusoe have to do to survive on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. How was Crusoe able to build a home and tools on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What kinds of challenges did Crusoe face while living on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Action vs. Linking Verbs

What is it?
Nouns are words that name people, places, things, or ideas. They can be proper nouns (specific names) or common nouns (general names).

Example from today's reading:
"In the story, some nouns used are 'island', 'home', 'tools', and 'animals'."

Your Turn:

Find five verbs in today's reading and label each as action or linking.

Page 17

Narrative Writing

Prompt:

Imagine you are Aunt Em in the cellar. Write about what you're thinking as the storm passes overhead.

Remember: Write a short story or scene with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Include descriptive details and dialogue if appropriate.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Idioms and Figurative Language

Learn:
Idioms are phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say (like 'raining cats and dogs').

Activity:

Identify any idioms or figurative language in today's chapter and explain what they really mean.

Excellent Work!

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 159! Come back tomorrow to continue reading Heidi.

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

Do you think Robinson Crusoe made the right decision to stay on the island instead of trying to leave? Why or why not?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 11]

Page 11

Informational Text

Surviving on a Deserted Island

Part 1 of 2

Imagine being shipwrecked on a remote island, with no one but yourself to rely on. This is the reality that Robinson Crusoe faces in the classic novel. As he begins his journey of survival, Crusoe must learn to adapt to his new island home and use his resourcefulness to thrive in this unfamiliar environment.

Page 12

Surviving on a Deserted Island

Part 2 of 2

From building a sturdy shelter to finding sources of food and water, Crusoe must navigate the challenges of island life. His story highlights the importance of problem-solving and self-reliance in the face of adversity. Through his experiences, readers can learn valuable lessons about the power of determination and the human spirit to overcome obstacles.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. What are some of the challenges Crusoe faces in adapting to island life?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. How does Crusoe's resourcefulness help him survive on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What lessons can readers learn from Crusoe's experiences on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Past Progressive Tense

What is it?
Adverbs are words that modify verbs, adjectives, or other adverbs. They can describe how, when, where, or to what extent an action is performed.

Example from today's reading:
"In the story, Crusoe works diligently to build his shelter and carefully gathers supplies to sustain himself on the island."

Your Turn:

Write three sentences using the past progressive tense to describe things happening in today's chapter.

Page 17

Informative Writing

Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

Remember: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Similes and Metaphors

Learn:
A simile compares two things using 'like' or 'as'. A metaphor says one thing IS another thing.

Activity:

Find the simile in today's reading or create your own simile about the story.

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 161!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 161! Come back tomorrow to continue reading Heidi.

Page 1

Lesson 162

3rd Grade ELA

Heidi

Chapter 12

Page 2

Welcome to Lesson 162!

Today you will:

  • Read from The Secret Garden
  • Learn 2 new vocabulary words
  • Read informational text: "Related Non-Fiction"
  • Practice grammar: Prepositional Phrases
  • Write: Narrative Writing
  • Explore: Context Clues

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

Page 3

Vocabulary Words

moorland

Look up this word in a dictionary, then write the definition in your own words:

0 / 10 words
tame

Look up this word in a dictionary, then write the definition in your own words:

0 / 10 words

Watch for these words in today's reading!

Page 4

Reading: The Secret Garden

Chapter 12

Page 1 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Secret Garden - Chapter 12, Part 1

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Book: The Secret Garden by Frances Hodgson Burnett

Page 5

Page 2 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Secret Garden - Chapter 12, Part 2

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 6

Page 3 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Secret Garden - Chapter 12, Part 3

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 7

Page 4 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Secret Garden - Chapter 12, Part 4

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 8

Page 5 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Secret Garden - Chapter 12, Part 5

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What did Robinson Crusoe do to make his home more comfortable on the island?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

Why do you think Crusoe decided to build a boat, even though he couldn't sail it?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

Do you think Crusoe made the right decision to stay on the island instead of trying to leave? Explain your answer.

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 12]

Page 11

Informational Text

Adapting to Island Life: How Robinson Crusoe Thrived

Part 1 of 2

When Robinson Crusoe found himself stranded on a deserted island, he had to quickly adapt to his new environment. With no modern amenities, Crusoe had to rely on his own resourcefulness and creativity to survive. He built a shelter, foraged for food, and even tamed wild animals to help him with his daily tasks.

Page 12

Adapting to Island Life: How Robinson Crusoe Thrived

Part 2 of 2

As the days turned into weeks and months, Crusoe became increasingly skilled at navigating the island. He learned to cultivate crops, construct tools, and even create his own calendar to keep track of time. Through his determination and resilience, Crusoe not only survived but thrived on the island, demonstrating the incredible power of the human spirit.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. How did Robinson Crusoe adapt to life on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. What were some of the challenges Crusoe faced in his new environment?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What skills did Crusoe develop to help him survive and thrive on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Prepositional Phrases

What is it?
Adverbs are words that describe verbs, adjectives, or other adverbs. They provide more information about how an action is performed.

Example from today's reading:
"In the story, Crusoe 'quickly' adapted to his new environment, demonstrating the use of an adverb to describe the manner in which he adapted."

Your Turn:

Find three prepositional phrases in today's reading and circle the preposition in each one.

Page 17

Narrative Writing

Prompt:

Imagine you are Aunt Em in the cellar. Write about what you're thinking as the storm passes overhead.

Remember: Write a short story or scene with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Include descriptive details and dialogue if appropriate.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Context Clues

Learn:
Use surrounding words to figure out the meaning of an unfamiliar word.

Activity:

Find an unfamiliar word in the text and use context clues to determine its meaning before looking it up.

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 162!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 162! Come back tomorrow to continue reading Heidi.

Lesson 163

3rd Grade ELA

Welcome to Lesson 163!

Today you will:

  • Read from The Secret Garden
  • Learn 2 new vocabulary words
  • Read informational text: "Related Non-Fiction"
  • Practice grammar: Compound Sentences
  • Write: Opinion Writing
  • Explore: Root Words and Affixes

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

Reading: The Secret Garden

Chapter 13

Page 4 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Secret Garden - Chapter 13, Part 4

Page 8

Page 5 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Secret Garden - Chapter 13, Part 5

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What did Robinson Crusoe do to make his home more comfortable on the island?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

Why do you think Crusoe was so afraid of the footprint he found on the sand?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

Do you think Crusoe should have tried to find the person who made the footprint? Why or why not?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 13]

Page 11

Informational Text

Thriving on a Deserted Island

Part 1 of 2

Imagine being stranded on a deserted island, with no one to rely on but yourself. This is the reality that Robinson Crusoe faces in the story. Despite being alone, he must use his resourcefulness and survival skills to make the most of his situation. From building shelter to finding food and water, Crusoe demonstrates the incredible resilience of the human spirit.

Page 12

Thriving on a Deserted Island

Part 2 of 2

As the days turn into weeks and months, Crusoe learns to adapt to his island home. He discovers ways to cultivate the land, create tools, and even domesticate animals. Through his determination and ingenuity, Crusoe not only survives but begins to thrive in his isolated environment. His story serves as an inspiration to us all, reminding us that even in the most challenging circumstances, we have the power to overcome and create a life for ourselves.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. How does Crusoe demonstrate his resourcefulness in the early days of being stranded on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. What are some of the ways Crusoe learns to adapt and thrive on the island over time?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What qualities or skills does Crusoe possess that help him survive and succeed on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Compound Sentences

What is it?
Adjectives are words that describe nouns. They can be used to provide more detailed and vivid descriptions in our writing.

Example from today's reading:
"In the story, Crusoe encounters various plants and animals on the island, which he describes as 'lush', 'exotic', and 'fascinating'."

Your Turn:

Write three compound sentences about today's chapter using different coordinating conjunctions.

Page 17

Opinion Writing

Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

Remember: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Root Words and Affixes

Learn:
Many words are built from roots (base words) with prefixes (before) and suffixes (after) added.

Activity:

Break down vocabulary words into their parts: prefix + root + suffix. Example: uncomfortable = un + comfort + able

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 163!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 163! Come back tomorrow to continue reading Heidi.

Page 5 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Secret Garden - Chapter 14, Part 5

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What is the name of the island where Robinson Crusoe is stranded?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

How does Crusoe feel about being alone on the island?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

Why do you think Crusoe decides to build a shelter on the island?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 14]

Page 11

Informational Text

Surviving on a Deserted Island

Part 1 of 2

Imagine being shipwrecked on a deserted island, like Robinson Crusoe. How would you survive? You'd need to find food, water, and shelter to live. Gathering resources from the island would be essential. You'd have to be very resourceful to make tools and other supplies from the materials around you.

Page 12

Surviving on a Deserted Island

Part 2 of 2

Robinson Crusoe built a shelter, grew crops, and even made pottery to help him live on the island. He had to use his creativity and problem-solving skills to figure out how to meet his basic needs. Surviving on a deserted island requires a lot of determination and adaptability.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. What are some of the resources Robinson Crusoe would have had to find on the island to survive?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. How did Robinson Crusoe demonstrate resourcefulness in his situation?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What skills do you think would be most important for someone stranded on a deserted island?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Action vs. Linking Verbs

What is it?
Conjunctions are words that connect two ideas or sentences. Common conjunctions include 'and', 'but', 'because', and 'so'.

Example from today's reading:
"In the story, Robinson Crusoe says 'I made me a table, but I had no chair.'"

Your Turn:

Find five verbs in today's reading and label each as action or linking.

Page 17

Informative Writing

Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

Remember: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Idioms and Figurative Language

Learn:
Idioms are phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say (like 'raining cats and dogs').

Activity:

Identify any idioms or figurative language in today's chapter and explain what they really mean.

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 164!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 164! Come back tomorrow to continue reading Heidi.

Day 165: Day 165 Assessment - 3rd Grade BedrockELA

Page 1

Lesson 165

Assessment Day

Heidi

Page 2

Assessment Instructions

Today is an assessment day! You'll be tested on:

  • Vocabulary: 10 words from Days 161-165
  • Reading Comprehension: Questions about the chapters
  • Grammar: Adjectives and Adjective Order
  • Writing: Narrative Writing

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

Tip: Take your time and do your best work!

Page 3

Vocabulary Assessment

Write the definition for each word. Use complete sentences.

1. delightful

2. humble

3. radiant

4. miracle

5. terrific

6. solemn

7. earnest

8. whisper

9. struggle

10. comfort

Page 4

Reading Comprehension Test

Answer these questions about the chapters you read this week:

Question 1:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 2:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 3:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 4:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 5:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Page 5

Grammar Assessment

Adjectives and Adjective Order

Write three sentences with multiple adjectives describing characters or places from the chapter.

Page 6

Writing Assessment

Narrative Writing

Prompt:

Imagine you are Aunt Em in the cellar. Write about what you're thinking as the storm passes overhead.

Requirements: Write a short story or scene with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Include descriptive details and dialogue if appropriate.

Page 7

Assessment Complete!

Great work on your assessment! You've completed 5 full days of Heidi.

See you on Day 166!

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What was the most important thing that Robinson Crusoe found on the island that helped him survive?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

How do you think Robinson Crusoe felt when he realized he was the only person on the island?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

Why do you think Robinson Crusoe decided to keep a journal of his time on the island?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 16]

Page 11

Informational Text

Surviving on a Desert Island

Part 1 of 2

When Robinson Crusoe found himself stranded on a desert island, he had to rely on his own resourcefulness and ingenuity to survive. With no other humans around, he had to figure out how to build shelter, find food and water, and protect himself from the elements.

Page 12

Surviving on a Desert Island

Part 2 of 2

Crusoe used the materials he found on the island to create tools and other necessary items. He built a home out of wood and leaves, and he learned to hunt and fish for his food. Crusoe also had to be creative in his problem-solving, finding ways to make the best use of the limited resources available to him.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. What are some of the ways Crusoe used his resourcefulness to survive on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. How did Crusoe's isolation on the island affect his daily life and decision-making?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What challenges do you think Crusoe faced in adapting to life on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Past Progressive Tense

What is it?
Adjectives are words that describe nouns. They can be used to provide more detail and color to the story.

Example from today's reading:
"In the story, Crusoe described the island as a 'desolate' and 'uninhabited' place, using adjectives to paint a vivid picture for the reader."

Your Turn:

Write three sentences using the past progressive tense to describe things happening in today's chapter.

Page 17

Opinion Writing

Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

Remember: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Similes and Metaphors

Learn:
A simile compares two things using 'like' or 'as'. A metaphor says one thing IS another thing.

Activity:

Find the simile in today's reading or create your own simile about the story.

Excellent Work!

Page 5 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Secret Garden - Chapter 17, Part 5

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What did Robinson Crusoe do to make his island home more comfortable?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

How did Robinson Crusoe's feelings about being alone on the island change over time?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

What do you think was the most important lesson Robinson Crusoe learned during his time on the island?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 17]

Page 11

Informational Text

Surviving on a Deserted Island

Part 1 of 2

Imagine being stranded on a deserted island, with nothing but the clothes on your back and the surrounding nature to rely on. This is the reality that Robinson Crusoe faced when he was shipwrecked on a remote island. Forced to adapt to his new environment, Crusoe had to learn how to build shelter, find food and water, and protect himself from the elements.

Page 12

Surviving on a Deserted Island

Part 2 of 2

As days turned into weeks and months, Crusoe's resourcefulness and determination were put to the test. He scavenged materials from the wrecked ship, crafted tools, and cultivated the land to create a sustainable way of life. Through his actions, Crusoe demonstrated the human spirit's ability to overcome adversity and thrive in even the most challenging circumstances.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. What were some of the challenges Crusoe faced when he was stranded on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. How did Crusoe use his resourcefulness to survive on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What skills or qualities do you think were important for Crusoe to develop in order to survive on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Prepositional Phrases

What is it?
Nouns are words that name people, places, things, or ideas. In the story of Robinson Crusoe, we can find many different nouns, such as island, ship, shelter, and tools.

Example from today's reading:
"Can you identify the nouns in this sentence from the story: 'Crusoe scavenged materials from the wrecked ship to build his shelter.'"

Your Turn:

Find three prepositional phrases in today's reading and circle the preposition in each one.

Page 17

Informative Writing

Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

Remember: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Context Clues

Learn:
Use surrounding words to figure out the meaning of an unfamiliar word.

Activity:

Find an unfamiliar word in the text and use context clues to determine its meaning before looking it up.

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 167!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 167! Come back tomorrow to continue reading Heidi.

📚 Placeholder for The Secret Garden - Chapter 18, Part 3

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 7

Page 4 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Secret Garden - Chapter 18, Part 4

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 8

Page 5 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Secret Garden - Chapter 18, Part 5

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What does Robinson Crusoe use to make his new home more comfortable?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

How does Crusoe's attitude towards his situation change over time?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

Why do you think Crusoe decides to build a fence around his home?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 18]

Page 11

Informational Text

Surviving on a Deserted Island: Lessons from Robinson Crusoe

Part 1 of 2

Imagine being stranded on a deserted island, with nothing but the clothes on your back and the resources around you. This is the reality that Robinson Crusoe faced when he was shipwrecked on an uninhabited island. Through his perseverance and ingenuity, Crusoe was able to not only survive, but thrive in his island home.

Page 12

Surviving on a Deserted Island: Lessons from Robinson Crusoe

Part 2 of 2

As Crusoe built his shelter, cultivated his land, and created tools from the materials available, he demonstrated the true power of resourcefulness. Each challenge he faced, from finding food to protecting himself from the elements, was an opportunity for him to showcase his adaptability and problem-solving skills. His story serves as a testament to the human spirit and the remarkable resilience we can possess when faced with adversity.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. What were some of the key resources Crusoe used to survive on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. How did Crusoe's resourcefulness and problem-solving skills help him overcome the challenges of island life?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What lessons can we learn from Crusoe's experiences about the importance of adaptability and resilience?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Compound Sentences

What is it?
Proper nouns, such as names of people, places, and organizations, should always be capitalized. This helps to clearly identify them in a sentence.

Example from today's reading:
"Robinson Crusoe carefully explored the island, searching for a suitable location to build his shelter."

Your Turn:

Write three compound sentences about today's chapter using different coordinating conjunctions.

Page 17

Narrative Writing

Prompt:

Imagine you are Aunt Em in the cellar. Write about what you're thinking as the storm passes overhead.

Remember: Write a short story or scene with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Include descriptive details and dialogue if appropriate.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Root Words and Affixes

Learn:
Many words are built from roots (base words) with prefixes (before) and suffixes (after) added.

Activity:

Break down vocabulary words into their parts: prefix + root + suffix. Example: uncomfortable = un + comfort + able

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 168!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 168! Come back tomorrow to continue reading Heidi.

  • Read informational text: "Related Non-Fiction"

Reading: The Secret Garden

Page 4 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Secret Garden - Chapter 19, Part 4

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 8

Page 5 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Secret Garden - Chapter 19, Part 5

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What did Robinson Crusoe do to make his home more comfortable on the island?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

Why do you think Robinson Crusoe decided to build a boat, even though he had no way to launch it?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

Do you think Robinson Crusoe was wise to spend so much time building the boat? Why or why not?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 19]

Page 11

Informational Text

Resourceful Living on a Deserted Island

Part 1 of 2

Being stranded on a deserted island can be a daunting experience, but as Robinson Crusoe has discovered, it can also be an opportunity to showcase one's resourcefulness. With limited supplies and no access to modern conveniences, Crusoe has had to rely on his wits and creativity to survive and thrive on the island. From building a sturdy shelter to crafting tools and hunting for food, he has demonstrated an impressive ability to adapt to his new environment.

Page 12

Resourceful Living on a Deserted Island

Part 2 of 2

Crusoe's ingenuity and determination have been vital to his survival. He has found innovative ways to make use of the resources available to him, such as using animal skins to create clothing and building a raft to explore the surrounding waters. His willingness to experiment and try new approaches has allowed him to overcome the challenges he has faced, and his story serves as a testament to the human spirit's resilience and adaptability.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. How has Crusoe's resourcefulness helped him to survive on the deserted island?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. What are some of the creative ways Crusoe has used the island's resources to meet his needs?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What do you think has been the most important factor in Crusoe's ability to adapt to his new environment?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Action vs. Linking Verbs

What is it?
Adverbs are words that modify verbs, adjectives, or other adverbs. They can describe how an action is performed, such as quickly, carefully, or reluctantly.

Example from today's reading:
"Crusoe quickly built a shelter to protect himself from the elements, and he carefully gathered supplies to ensure his survival."

Your Turn:

Find five verbs in today's reading and label each as action or linking.

Page 17

Opinion Writing

Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

Remember: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Idioms and Figurative Language

Learn:
Idioms are phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say (like 'raining cats and dogs').

Activity:

Identify any idioms or figurative language in today's chapter and explain what they really mean.

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 169!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 169! Come back tomorrow to continue reading Heidi.

Day 170: Day 170 Assessment - 3rd Grade BedrockELA

Page 1

Lesson 170

Assessment Day

Heidi

Page 2

Assessment Instructions

Today is an assessment day! You'll be tested on:

  • Vocabulary: 10 words from Days 166-170
  • Reading Comprehension: Questions about the chapters
  • Grammar: Adjectives and Adjective Order
  • Writing: Informative Writing

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

Tip: Take your time and do your best work!

Page 3

Vocabulary Assessment

Write the definition for each word. Use complete sentences.

1. delightful

2. humble

3. radiant

4. miracle

5. terrific

6. solemn

7. earnest

8. whisper

9. struggle

10. comfort

Page 4

Reading Comprehension Test

Answer these questions about the chapters you read this week:

Question 1:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 2:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 3:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 4:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 5:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Page 5

Grammar Assessment

Adjectives and Adjective Order

Write three sentences with multiple adjectives describing characters or places from the chapter.

Page 6

Writing Assessment

Informative Writing

Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

Requirements: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

Page 7

Assessment Complete!

Great work on your assessment! You've completed 5 full days of Heidi.

See you on Day 171!

Page 4 of 5

Page 8

Page 5 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Secret Garden - Chapter 21, Part 5

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What did Robinson Crusoe do to improve his shelter on the island?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

Why do you think Robinson Crusoe was so determined to make his home on the island as comfortable as possible?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

If you were in Robinson Crusoe's situation, what would you have done differently to make your life on the island more enjoyable?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 21]

Page 11

Informational Text

Adapting to Island Life: Crusoe's Resourcefulness

Part 1 of 2

After being stranded on the island for nearly half a year, Robinson Crusoe has had to adapt to his new environment in order to survive. He has learned to build shelter, hunt for food, and create tools from the limited resources available on the island. Crusoe's ability to problem-solve and make the most of his surroundings has been crucial to his survival.

Page 12

Adapting to Island Life: Crusoe's Resourcefulness

Part 2 of 2

One of the most remarkable aspects of Crusoe's island life is his resourcefulness. He has fashioned everything from clothes to furniture using only the materials he can find. Crusoe has even managed to grow crops and domesticate animals, creating a semi-self-sufficient lifestyle on the remote island. His determination and ingenuity have been the keys to his success in this isolated environment.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. How has Crusoe adapted to life on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. What are some examples of Crusoe's resourcefulness?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. Why has Crusoe's ability to problem-solve been so important to his survival?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Past Progressive Tense

What is it?
Adverbs of manner describe how an action is performed. They often end in '-ly' and can provide more detail about Crusoe's actions on the island.

Example from today's reading:
"Crusoe carefully built his shelter to protect himself from the elements."

Your Turn:

Write three sentences using the past progressive tense to describe things happening in today's chapter.

Page 17

Narrative Writing

Prompt:

Imagine you are Aunt Em in the cellar. Write about what you're thinking as the storm passes overhead.

Remember: Write a short story or scene with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Include descriptive details and dialogue if appropriate.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Similes and Metaphors

Learn:
A simile compares two things using 'like' or 'as'. A metaphor says one thing IS another thing.

Activity:

Find the simile in today's reading or create your own simile about the story.

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 171!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 171! Come back tomorrow to continue reading Heidi.

📚 Placeholder for The Secret Garden - Chapter 22, Part 4

Page 8

Page 5 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Secret Garden - Chapter 22, Part 5

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What did Robinson Crusoe do to prepare for the rainy season?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

Why did Robinson Crusoe decide to build a new shelter?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

Do you think Robinson Crusoe was wise to build a new shelter? Why or why not?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 22]

Page 11

Informational Text

Finding Food and Shelter on a Desert Island

Part 1 of 2

When Robinson Crusoe found himself alone on a deserted island, he knew he would need to rely on his resourcefulness and survival skills to make it through. One of his first challenges was finding a reliable source of food and water. He scoured the island, searching for edible plants and any signs of wildlife that could provide sustenance.

Page 12

Finding Food and Shelter on a Desert Island

Part 2 of 2

After securing a steady food supply, Crusoe turned his attention to building a shelter. Using the materials he had on hand, such as branches, leaves, and even animal hides, he constructed a small hut that would protect him from the elements. This shelter not only provided him with a place to rest but also served as a secure base from which he could explore the island further.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. What were some of the first things Crusoe had to do to survive on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. How did Crusoe use the resources around him to build a shelter?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. Why was it important for Crusoe to have a secure base on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Prepositional Phrases

What is it?
Compound sentences are made up of two independent clauses joined by a coordinating conjunction (e.g., and, but, or, yet).

Example from today's reading:
"Crusoe searched the island for food, and he found several edible plants."

Your Turn:

Find three prepositional phrases in today's reading and circle the preposition in each one.

Page 17

Opinion Writing

Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

Remember: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Context Clues

Learn:
Use surrounding words to figure out the meaning of an unfamiliar word.

Activity:

Find an unfamiliar word in the text and use context clues to determine its meaning before looking it up.

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 172!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 172! Come back tomorrow to continue reading Heidi.

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What did Robinson Crusoe do to make his home more comfortable?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

How did Robinson Crusoe's attitude towards the island change over time?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

What do you think was the most important lesson Robinson Crusoe learned during his time on the island?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 23]

Page 11

Informational Text

Resourceful Survival: Thriving on a Deserted Island

Part 1 of 2

Being shipwrecked on a remote island can be a daunting experience, but as Robinson Crusoe discovers, resourcefulness and determination are the keys to survival. With limited supplies and no immediate rescue in sight, Crusoe must rely on his ingenuity to build shelter, find food and water, and establish a self-sufficient way of life.

Page 12

Resourceful Survival: Thriving on a Deserted Island

Part 2 of 2

Through his unwavering resilience, Crusoe transforms the barren island into a sanctuary, crafting tools, cultivating crops, and even domesticating animals. His story serves as a testament to the human spirit's ability to adapt and thrive in the face of adversity, inspiring readers to find strength within themselves when faced with challenging circumstances.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. What strategies does Crusoe use to survive on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. How does Crusoe's resourcefulness help him establish a sustainable lifestyle?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What lessons can readers learn from Crusoe's experience of adapting to island life?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Compound Sentences

What is it?
In this chapter, we see many proper nouns, such as the names of people, places, and specific objects. It's important to capitalize proper nouns to ensure they are clearly identified.

Example from today's reading:
"Robinson Crusoe, the island, the raft, the cave"

Your Turn:

Write three compound sentences about today's chapter using different coordinating conjunctions.

Page 17

Informative Writing

Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

Remember: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Root Words and Affixes

Learn:
Many words are built from roots (base words) with prefixes (before) and suffixes (after) added.

Activity:

Break down vocabulary words into their parts: prefix + root + suffix. Example: uncomfortable = un + comfort + able

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

Why do you think Robinson Crusoe decided to make a new home in the cave?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

What do you think was the most important thing that Robinson Crusoe made or built in the cave? Explain your answer.

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 24]

Page 11

Informational Text

Surviving on a Deserted Island: Lessons from Robinson Crusoe

Part 1 of 2

In Daniel Defoe's classic novel 'Robinson Crusoe,' the protagonist finds himself stranded on a remote island, forced to rely on his own resourcefulness and ingenuity to survive. As Crusoe navigates the challenges of island life, he demonstrates the importance of adaptability, problem-solving, and making the most of the resources at hand.

Page 12

Surviving on a Deserted Island: Lessons from Robinson Crusoe

Part 2 of 2

Crusoe's story highlights the human capacity for resilience and self-reliance. From building a sturdy shelter to cultivating crops and taming wild animals, his actions showcase the remarkable ways in which individuals can overcome adversity and thrive in even the most isolated and harsh environments.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. How does Crusoe's approach to survival on the island reflect his resourcefulness and problem-solving abilities?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. What specific challenges does Crusoe face, and how does he overcome them?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What lessons about self-reliance and adaptability can readers learn from Crusoe's experiences on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Action vs. Linking Verbs

What is it?
Adverbs of manner describe how an action is performed. They often end in '-ly' and provide additional information about the verb.

Example from today's reading:
"In the book, Crusoe 'skillfully' built his shelter, demonstrating his adept craftsmanship."

Your Turn:

Find five verbs in today's reading and label each as action or linking.

Page 17

Narrative Writing

Prompt:

Imagine you are Aunt Em in the cellar. Write about what you're thinking as the storm passes overhead.

Remember: Write a short story or scene with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Include descriptive details and dialogue if appropriate.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Idioms and Figurative Language

Learn:
Idioms are phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say (like 'raining cats and dogs').

Activity:

Identify any idioms or figurative language in today's chapter and explain what they really mean.

Excellent Work!

Day 175: Day 175 Assessment - 3rd Grade BedrockELA

Page 1

Lesson 175

Assessment Day

Heidi

Page 2

Assessment Instructions

Today is an assessment day! You'll be tested on:

  • Vocabulary: 10 words from Days 171-175
  • Reading Comprehension: Questions about the chapters
  • Grammar: Adjectives and Adjective Order
  • Writing: Opinion Writing

Time needed: About 45-60 minutes

Tip: Take your time and do your best work!

Page 3

Vocabulary Assessment

Write the definition for each word. Use complete sentences.

1. delightful

2. humble

3. radiant

4. miracle

5. terrific

6. solemn

7. earnest

8. whisper

9. struggle

10. comfort

Page 4

Reading Comprehension Test

Answer these questions about the chapters you read this week:

Question 1:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 2:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 3:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 4:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Question 5:

[Comprehension question about this week's chapters]

Page 5

Grammar Assessment

Adjectives and Adjective Order

Write three sentences with multiple adjectives describing characters or places from the chapter.

Page 6

Writing Assessment

Opinion Writing

Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

Requirements: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

Page 7

Assessment Complete!

Great work on your assessment! You've completed 5 full days of Heidi.

See you on Day 176!

Page 5 of 5

📚 Placeholder for The Secret Garden - Chapter 26, Part 5

This page will contain approximately 500 words from the chapter when book text is integrated. The formatting is ready: 11px font, line-height 1.2, ~5 pages per chapter.

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What did Robinson Crusoe use to make a shelter for himself on the island?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

Why do you think Crusoe was so relieved to find the footprint in the sand?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

How do you think Crusoe's feelings changed when he realized he was not alone on the island?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 26]

Page 11

Informational Text

Surviving on a Deserted Island

Part 1 of 2

Being stranded on a deserted island can be a challenging and daunting experience, but it also presents opportunities for resourcefulness and survival. As Robinson Crusoe discovered, it's important to find shelter, secure a reliable source of food and water, and create tools to make life on the island more manageable.

Page 12

Surviving on a Deserted Island

Part 2 of 2

Crusoe used his ingenuity to build a makeshift shelter, grow crops, and create various tools and implements to aid in his survival. He also learned to adapt to the island's environment and find ways to make the most of the resources available to him, demonstrating the power of resilience and determination.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. What are some of the key challenges Crusoe faced in surviving on the deserted island?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. How did Crusoe demonstrate resourcefulness in his efforts to survive?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What steps did Crusoe take to ensure he had a reliable source of food and water on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Past Progressive Tense

What is it?
Compound sentences are made up of two or more independent clauses joined by a conjunction, such as 'and', 'but', or 'or'.

Example from today's reading:
"Crusoe built a shelter, and he also planted crops to ensure a reliable food source."

Your Turn:

Write three sentences using the past progressive tense to describe things happening in today's chapter.

Page 17

Informative Writing

Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

Remember: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Similes and Metaphors

Learn:
A simile compares two things using 'like' or 'as'. A metaphor says one thing IS another thing.

Activity:

Find the simile in today's reading or create your own simile about the story.

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 176!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 176! Come back tomorrow to continue reading Heidi.

Page 9

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

Why did Robinson Crusoe decide to make a boat to get off the island?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

What do you think Robinson Crusoe was feeling when he realized he could not move the boat he made to the water?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

If you were Robinson Crusoe, what other ways would you have tried to escape the island?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 27]

Page 11

Informational Text

Thriving on a Deserted Island

Part 1 of 2

Robinson Crusoe has been stranded on a remote island for 177 days. Despite the challenges, he has learned to adapt and thrive in his new environment. Using his resourcefulness and ingenuity, Crusoe has built a shelter, grown crops, and even created tools to make his life more comfortable.

Page 12

Thriving on a Deserted Island

Part 2 of 2

One of the keys to Crusoe's survival has been his ability to make the most of the resources around him. He has learned to hunt, fish, and forage for food, and has even managed to create a makeshift farming system to grow his own crops. Crusoe's resilience and determination have been essential in helping him survive on the island.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. How has Crusoe been able to survive on the island for so long?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. What are some of the ways Crusoe has used his resources to make his life more comfortable?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What do you think has been the biggest challenge for Crusoe in his time on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Prepositional Phrases

What is it?
Compound sentences are made up of two independent clauses joined by a conjunction (e.g., and, but, or).

Example from today's reading:
"Crusoe built a shelter, and he also grew crops to sustain himself."

Your Turn:

Find three prepositional phrases in today's reading and circle the preposition in each one.

Page 17

Narrative Writing

Prompt:

Imagine you are Aunt Em in the cellar. Write about what you're thinking as the storm passes overhead.

Remember: Write a short story or scene with a clear beginning, middle, and end. Include descriptive details and dialogue if appropriate.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Context Clues

Learn:
Use surrounding words to figure out the meaning of an unfamiliar word.

Activity:

Find an unfamiliar word in the text and use context clues to determine its meaning before looking it up.

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 177!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 177! Come back tomorrow to continue reading Heidi.

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What item did Robinson Crusoe find that helped him make a shelter?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

How did Robinson Crusoe's daily routine change after he found the cave?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

Do you think Robinson Crusoe was wise to make the cave his new home? Explain your answer.

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 28]

Page 11

Informational Text

Crafting Tools and Shelter: Resourcefulness on the Island

Part 1 of 2

After being shipwrecked on a deserted island, Robinson Crusoe quickly realized that he would need to rely on his own resourcefulness and ingenuity to survive. With limited supplies, he set out to create the necessary tools and shelter to sustain himself. Using materials found on the island, he crafted a sturdy shelter, fashioned a knife from a piece of iron, and even built a small table and chair.

Page 12

Crafting Tools and Shelter: Resourcefulness on the Island

Part 2 of 2

Crusoe's determination and problem-solving skills were put to the test as he navigated the challenges of island life. He learned to hunt and forage for food, utilizing the natural resources around him. His ability to adapt and make the most of his circumstances allowed him to establish a self-sufficient lifestyle, laying the foundation for his long-term survival on the island.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. What were some of the tools and items that Crusoe crafted to help him survive on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. How did Crusoe's resourcefulness and problem-solving skills contribute to his ability to thrive on the island?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. What were some of the challenges Crusoe faced in adapting to island life, and how did he overcome them?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Compound Sentences

What is it?
Conjunctions are words that connect two or more ideas in a sentence. They help to show the relationship between the ideas.

Example from today's reading:
"In the book, Crusoe uses conjunctions like 'and' and 'but' to connect his thoughts and describe his experiences on the island."

Your Turn:

Write three compound sentences about today's chapter using different coordinating conjunctions.

Page 17

Opinion Writing

Prompt:

Should Dorothy have tried harder to get into the cellar, or was it okay that she stopped to get Toto? Defend your opinion.

Remember: Write a paragraph stating your opinion with at least 2-3 reasons supporting your view. Include a topic sentence and conclusion.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Root Words and Affixes

Learn:
Many words are built from roots (base words) with prefixes (before) and suffixes (after) added.

Activity:

Break down vocabulary words into their parts: prefix + root + suffix. Example: uncomfortable = un + comfort + able

Excellent Work!

You've completed all 11 pages of Lesson 178!

Page 19

Great Job!

You've completed Lesson 178! Come back tomorrow to continue reading Heidi.

Reading Comprehension

Answer these questions about today's chapter:

Question 1:

What did Robinson Crusoe use to make a ladder to climb up the rock?

0 / 20 words

Question 2:

How did Robinson Crusoe feel when he realized he could not get down from the top of the rock?

0 / 20 words

Question 3:

Why do you think Robinson Crusoe decided to build a shelter on top of the rock?

0 / 20 words

Page 10

Journal Reflection

[Personal reflection prompt related to Chapter 29]

Page 11

Informational Text

How to Survive on a Deserted Island

Part 1 of 2

Being stranded on a deserted island can be a challenging and daunting experience. However, with the right mindset and resourcefulness, you can not only survive but thrive in this type of environment. One of the key things to focus on is finding a reliable source of food and water. This may involve foraging for edible plants, hunting small animals, or collecting rainwater.

Page 12

How to Survive on a Deserted Island

Part 2 of 2

Another important aspect of island life is shelter. Building a sturdy and weatherproof shelter can protect you from the elements and provide a safe haven. You may need to gather materials such as sticks, leaves, and branches to construct your shelter. Additionally, maintaining good hygiene and health is crucial, so finding ways to keep clean and treat any injuries or illnesses is essential for survival.

Page 13

Question 1 of 3

1. What are some of the key things to focus on when stranded on a deserted island?

0 / 15 words

Page 14

Question 2 of 3

2. How can you find a reliable source of food and water on a deserted island?

0 / 15 words

Page 15

Question 3 of 3

3. Why is maintaining good hygiene and health important for survival on a deserted island?

0 / 15 words

Page 16

Grammar Workshop

Action vs. Linking Verbs

What is it?
Adjectives are words that describe nouns. They can help add more detail and color to your writing.

Example from today's reading:
"In the book, Crusoe describes the island as 'vast' and 'desolate', using adjectives to paint a vivid picture for the reader."

Your Turn:

Find five verbs in today's reading and label each as action or linking.

Page 17

Informative Writing

Prompt:

Describe what life was like for Dorothy in Kansas before the cyclone. Include at least 3 specific details.

Remember: Write a paragraph explaining something from the story. Include clear details and examples.

Checklist: Did you include a topic sentence? Supporting details? A conclusion?

Page 18

Language Skills

Idioms and Figurative Language

Learn:
Idioms are phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say (like 'raining cats and dogs').

Activity:

Identify any idioms or figurative language in today's chapter and explain what they really mean.

Excellent Work!
